title: Add list command to pod command --help
body:
We had neglected to add the list command to the podman pod
command table.  It is an alias for ps.

Also do the same for the containers command while we're here.

This also required some hackery in our scripts that validate our
man pages during the CI runs.  Due to adding 'list' in a few places,
we came upon two hits of 'ps' in the man pages that the scripts
did not like.  Also, the scripts check the `help` output, and by
adding `list` there, things went south.

I had tried to tighten that logic up in those scripts, if you
have thoughts on making them better, I'd appreciate it.

My bash was very rusty, and my Perl was a bit rusty.  I made
good use of Gemini for the changes in the scripts.

Fixes: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-99879

comments.body:
Now the List command appears:

```
$ # podman pod --help
Manage pods

Description:
  Pods are a group of one or more containers sharing the same network, pid and ipc namespaces.

Usage:
  podman pod [command]

Available Commands:
  clone       Clone an existing pod
  create      Create a new empty pod
  exists      Check if a pod exists in local storage
  inspect     Display a pod configuration
  kill        Send the specified signal or SIGKILL to containers in pod
  list        List pods
  logs        Fetch logs for pod with one or more containers
  pause       Pause one or more pods
  prune       Remove all stopped pods and their containers
  ps          List pods
  restart     Restart one or more pods
  rm          Remove one or more pods
  start       Start one or more pods
  stats       Display a live stream of resource usage statistics for the containers in one or more pods
  stop        Stop one or more pods
  top         Display the running processes of containers in a pod
  unpause     Unpause one or more pods

```
This barfed on the missing link.  I'm not sure if the one I added is correct or not, we'll see.
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.
Well I keep fixing one Gremlin, and running into another, I'll give this a go again tomorrow.

I mean is the exception of that bug correct?

What is the difference between command and alias? Adding both here as duplicated of each other feels odd and backwards.
Generally listing all commands as duplicated in --help seem the opposite of useful, a naive users know things these are different commands when they are not.

would the more logical output be something like

```
ps (list, ls)         List pods
```
or something similar to that which correctly groups them as one command.
We do control the help output, see usageTemplate, so that may be possible to achieve then duplicating commands and aliases.

Keep in mind you only look podman pod but this very much is not a podman pod command problem, it applies to any command using aliases which is a lot so patching this one instance here by duplicating command structs seem not maintainable to me.

@Luap99  I concur on listing all the commands as not appropriate.  Personally, I think the list command is the one exception.   We have this BZ, and I've been queried a few times over the years by other folks.  A lot of systems use a "list" command rather than "ps", and I  always think "list" over "ps" when the command is an option to list an object in some software system.  I think others do as well.  

All that said, if we don't want to move forward with this, we can close it out, and I will just have enjoyed some playtime with Gemini, getting my Perl and Bash skills a little shinier.

> I think the list command is the one exception. We have this BZ, and I've been queried a few times over the years by other folks. A lot of systems use a "list" command rather than "ps", and I always think "list" over "ps" when the command is an option to list an object in some software system. I think others do as well.

I do not disagree there, `podman pod list`, `podman pod ls`, `podman pod ps` all work today already without this change.

What I disagree on is listing these as separate commands when they are not.

The amount of special cases this adds to our validation scripts make my head spin and falls into the category not worth the maintenance effort in my book.

Like I said I much rather see a proper general solution for showing aliases. Because nothing in this bug indicates that pod specific, whoever reported this might as well discover that `podman volume --help` does not show `list` either only `ls`. So then you say we close that all of the sudden?

I think aliases are useful and should just be properly marked in the help template but lets not add special cases for each and every command for this. Instead I like to see a one fix for all approach here.

Just had a short look, I am thinking in terms like this
```
$ git diff
diff --git a/cmd/podman/root.go b/cmd/podman/root.go
index fdbefe49d6..eb8675228c 100644
--- a/cmd/podman/root.go
+++ b/cmd/podman/root.go
@@ -70,7 +70,7 @@ Examples:
 {{.Example | indentExamples}}{{end}}{{if .HasAvailableSubCommands}}
 
 Available Commands:{{range .Commands}}{{if (or .IsAvailableCommand (eq .Name "help"))}}
-  {{rpad .Name .NamePadding }} {{.Short}}{{end}}{{end}}{{end}}{{if .HasAvailableLocalFlags}}
+  {{rpad .NameAndAliases .NamePadding }} {{.Short}}{{end}}{{end}}{{end}}{{if .HasAvailableLocalFlags}}
 
 Options:
 {{.LocalFlags.FlagUsages | trimTrailingWhitespaces}}{{end}}{{if .HasAvailableInheritedFlags}}
```

which produces something like that
```
  prune       Remove all stopped pods and their containers
  ps, ls, list List pods
  restart     Restart one or more pods
```

Of course indentation is wrong so we would have to write another function to take care of it but I think having them grouped into one line seems much better?
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
